omission.note: Direct quotes and author attributions — the highest-provenance claim types — were never examined.

self_sealing.reason: Both a working and a failing validator are interpreted as confirming the Epistemic Boundary, and no outcome is designated as disconfirming. As run, the hypothesis is self-sealing. (MarCognity's own Boundary doc is more careful — doc:epistemic_boundary L119-122 asks for controlled validation — so the self-sealing is in the forum conclusion, not necessarily the underlying construct.)
would_refute: A control showing the residual failures vanish under proper source-gating and provenance (i.e. the failures are a pipeline defect, not an 'intrinsic architecture' property) — NOT provided.
would_support: Validator flags unverifiable claims -> the confidence/verifiability gap is 'made visible' (the stated goal).
Validator fails / mis-attributes sources -> read as the Boundary 'even within the validator' (muse:L237).
would_weaken: A pre-registered outcome where the validator, given domain-correct sources, verifies claims with quoted passages — NOT provided.
